steps: Identify like terms. The \(yz^3\) terms are \(9yz^3\) and \(3yz^3\). The only \(yz\) term is \(6yz\).
Combine the \(yz^3\) terms: \(9yz^3 + 3yz^3 = 12yz^3\). The \(6yz\) term stays. Result: \(12yz^3 + 6yz\).
When combining like terms, exponents are NOT added — only coefficients add. So \(yz^3 + yz^3 = 2yz^3\), not \(yz^6\).

• A: \(12yz^3 + 6yz\) — Correct combination of like terms. That matches choice A — A is correct.
  • B: \(12yz^6 + 6yz\) — Incorrectly adds the exponents (\(3 + 3 = 6\)) instead of the coefficients. Eliminated.
  • C: \(18yz^6\) — Combines unlike terms and adds exponents incorrectly. Eliminated.
  • D: \(18yz^7\) — Combines all three terms as if they were like terms and adds all exponents. Eliminated.
